prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a7c3e91f0b4d28e6","BT":2,"BW":3,"HF":0.42,"RF":0.18,"MA":2,"hour_entropy":2.31,"behavioral_consistency":0.17,"response_latency_variance":5.24,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.83,"error_recovery_pattern":0.04,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.82,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.47,"gas_price_precision":0.68,"rationale":"Advanced evasion profile seeded at 7: all five HasciDB indicators kept well below flag thresholds while AI-discriminative features are pulled toward human-distribution means (low hour_entropy, low behavioral_consistency, high latency variance, low perplexity, moderate gas-precision) to also defeat the cross-axis 13-feature classifier."}
